campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. The council also discussed plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue and voted on extending a temporary local rule.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Plans moving forward for bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
City applying for grant funding to study the Hamilton Avenue bridge project
Extension of temporary local ordinance #2326

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the normal first reading step, with plans to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and expedited (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Standard approval process being accelerated

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve official records from several meetings held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details weren't fully outlined in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March meetings (both public and closed sessions)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (project details unclear from agenda)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several important agreements and projects. They renewed a contract with Vanderlande Industries to keep the airport baggage system running, approved protections for affordable housing if subsidies end, and hired Wood Rodgers to plan improvements to the city's storm sewer system. They also discussed the FEMA-funded family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage handling system contract renewal
Affordable housing protections during subsidy changes
Storm sewer system city-wide planning
FEMA family camp project updates
Committee meeting minutes approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on big infrastructure and improvement projects. They also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City planning and capital spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting began with the traditional flag salute and included special recognitions for these important community observances.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular City Council meeting proceedings
Community heritage celebrations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to help residents learn about and give feedback on OpenGov, a tool that shows the public how Cupertino spends its budget and manages money. The meeting also covered rules about who needs to register if they're trying to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool - public budget information access
Community input on city spending and finances
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city rules

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important information about your legal rights if you disagree with a city decision. If the city makes an official decision that affects you, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge—if you wait longer, you lose the right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal rights for residents disputing city rulings
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements
When decisions become 'final' and affect your timeline

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2030 and reviewed requests from council members for new work projects. The council also considered changes to how vacant council seats are filled and reviewed meeting schedules and minutes.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan amendments for housing and community development (2025-2030)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy filling process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of the building at 405 Kipling Street downtown, which would allow the owner to add 5,000 square feet of new space as a reward for preserving and improving the historic structure and making it safer against earthquakes. The Council also approved meeting notes from the Historic Resources Board's previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown commercial zoning with historic preservation incentives
